title: Lock layer: mutual exclusion fails under contention (Linux) + EPERM on Windows lock open
action: v1.8.0's exact-tag CI is RED on BOTH platforms; both failures are in the store-lock layer (skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/lock.mjs). (A) LINUX (run 29849652534, verify(18), 2-core): scripts/test_store_lock.mjs scenario (c) — '(c) run recorded 3 mutual-exclusion violation(s) — the lock did not exclude'. This is a REAL mutual-exclusion failure, already filed as backlog P2 today ('rare genuine mutual-exclusion violation in lock.mjs tryStaleTakeover under heavy contention', reproduced on unmodified baseline by the rel1710rc-3 worker). Scenario (c) pre-seeds a STALE lock and races racers through takeover — so the suspect is the stale-takeover path admitting two holders: the classic hole is takeover that checks staleness then acquires non-atomically, letting two racers both judge the same lock stale and both take it. Find the real ordering hole and make takeover atomic (a single O_EXCL-style claim of the takeover right, or an atomic rename of the stale lock into a holder-stamped name, so exactly one racer can win); mutual exclusion must hold with ZERO violations under contention. (B) WINDOWS (run 29849652492, portable-suites(20)): test_decisions_propagation 'race: log-vs-archive' fails with 'EPERM: operation not permitted, open C:\...\.bee\locks\decisions-*.lock'. Windows refuses to open/replace a file another handle holds — the exact class already fixed for claims.mjs in rel1710rc-5 via a bounded transient-fs retry (EBUSY/EPERM/ENOTEMPTY). The decisions store lock (shipped today in dp-3) goes through lock.mjs and never got that treatment. Apply the same bounded-retry discipline to lock.mjs's own open/rename/unlink operations — reuse the existing helper shape, do not invent a new one. RED-FIRST: for (A) force the interleaving deterministically (barrier/injected pause) so it fails 10/10 against current code — do NOT chase it with load; for (B) simulate the EPERM (stub the fs call to throw EPERM once, or use the same technique rel1710rc-5 used) so it fails without needing Windows. Record --passed false with both tails, then fix. Prove: test_store_lock 10/10 plain AND 10/10 under `taskset -c 0,1`; the forced-interleaving takeover test 10/10; test_decisions_propagation 10/10; full run_verify green 3 consecutive times.

truths: takeover is atomic — a forced-interleaving test fails 10/10 before the fix and passes 10/10 after, with ZERO mutual-exclusion violations
lock.mjs open/rename/unlink survive a transient EPERM/EBUSY via bounded retry, proven by a simulated-EPERM test (no Windows needed)
prohibitions: Never weaken or skip a mutual-exclusion assertion
No unbounded waiting — retries stay bounded like the existing precedent
Do not paper over (A) by making the test tolerant of violations

trace.outcome: Store lock now excludes under contention (takeover verifies the renamed-away content is the SAME stale lock it judged) and survives transient Windows EPERM via bounded retry
